site_context.name: Toquaht Bay
site_context.contamination_profile: Iron mine tailings -- elevated arsenic (naturally), moderate Cu/Zn, no organic contaminants (PAH/PCB never measured).
site_context.narrative: Toquaht Bay (Site 15184) is a former iron mine tailings area on the west coast of Vancouver Island. The dominant contamination pathway is metals. There is no organic contaminant pathway: PAHs and PCBs were never measured because they are not relevant at this site type. Toquaht has complete triad data at all 20 stations, making it the most data-rich site in the BN-RRM training corpus and an ideal case study for the site-scoped marginalization fix.

coverage_table.data_type: 7 Metals (Cu, Zn, Pb, Cd, Hg, As, Cr)

coverage_table.note: Not applicable -- iron mine site. No organic contaminant pathway.

key_gaps: No PAH/PCB data -- organic pathway entirely absent. This is expected for an iron mine site and is the reason the site-scoped marginalization override was needed.

fitting_approach.summary: Hierarchical borrowing from the general model with a site-scoped marginalization override for the organic pathway. Tier 1 CPTs borrowed wholesale; Tiers 2-4 use general CPTs as priors, refined by the 20 site stations. The marginalization override treats the missing organic_bioavail parent as uninformative (uniform 1/3 across states) rather than blocking all downstream observations.
fitting_approach.marginalization_scope: organic_bioavail parent of tox_amphipod and taxa_richness

site_scoped_overrides.rationale: organic_bioavail is NULL at all 20 Toquaht stations (no PAH/PCB data). Without marginalization, all 20 amphipod observations are discarded. The override distributes uniform weight (1/3) across the missing parent's states, allowing the metal pathway data to be learned.
Same as tox_amphipod: organic_bioavail is NULL at all 20 stations. Without marginalization, all 20 taxa richness observations are discarded.

cpt_tiers.data_status: Parent space too large for 20 stations -- general model priors used directly.

cpt_tiers.method: BDeu + general model priors, refined by site data
cpt_tiers.data_status: ~62% site-data-driven, ~38% general-model prior influence. organic_bioavail marginalized (no data).

cpt_tiers.method: BDeu + general model priors with marginalization override for organic_bioavail parent

before_after.change_description: Site-scoped Tier 3 marginalization fix for organic pathway. The fitting-policy rule was silently discarding 40 real toxicity and community observations because the organic pathway (PAH/PCB) had no measurements at this iron mine site.

before.label: Before Fix (row-dropping)
before.notes: All 20 amphipod and 20 taxa observations discarded because organic_bioavail was NULL. Model treated 'no organic data collected' as 'cannot learn about toxicity.' 6 stations incorrectly predicted as Moderate or High.

after.label: After Fix (site-scoped marginalization)
after.notes: All 20 amphipod and 20 taxa observations recovered via uniform marginalization of organic_bioavail. 5 stations corrected from Moderate to Low. 1 residual outlier (SED13-26b) remains.

residual_outliers.explanation: SED13-26b has one of two statistically significant amphipod effects at Toquaht (91% survival, sig=1), combined with low AVS (0.402 umol/g) and moderate TOC (2.0%). The model reads significant toxicity + low sulfide protection as potentially high-risk. This is a scientifically defensible interpretation even though the consultant's overall WOE assessment classified it as Low.

governance.scope_note: Site-scoped marginalization override for the organic pathway at Toquaht only. The general DAG structure (20 nodes, 24 edges) is unchanged. The general model's CPTs are completely unaffected. The organic_bioavail edge still exists in the DAG and will be used at sites that have organic data (e.g., ALCAN).

hitl_decisions.decision: Approved site-scoped marginalization of organic_bioavail parent for tox_amphipod and taxa_richness at Toquaht (Site 15184). Missing organic pathway data must not suppress observed biological effects from the metal pathway.

applicability_notes: Would NOT apply at organic-dominant sites like ALCAN where organic data is present.
Should not be applied where organic data was collected but is missing due to QA/QC failures -- only where the pathway is genuinely absent.
Each new site application of this marginalization override requires separate HITL review.

hitl_review_questions.question: Is the 95% LOO accuracy a meaningful result?
hitl_review_questions.context: With 20 stations all observed as Low, 19/20 correct is strong consistency. But the model has not been tested against Moderate or High risk at this site. The result demonstrates internal coherence, not predictive power across risk classes.

hitl_review_questions.question: Should SED13-26b be reviewed independently?
hitl_review_questions.context: The model's High prediction reflects a scientifically coherent reading of significant amphipod effects + low sulfide protection. The consultant's Low classification may reflect additional context not captured in the model (e.g., spatial patterns, reference comparisons).

hitl_review_questions.question: Should the marginalization override be applied to other sites?
hitl_review_questions.context: Only to sites where one pathway is genuinely absent (e.g., no PAH/PCB measurements). It should not be applied where data was collected but is missing due to QA/QC failures. Each site application requires HITL review.

hitl_review_questions.question: Does Toquaht's class homogeneity (all Low) limit the model's value?
hitl_review_questions.context: Yes, for classification accuracy benchmarking. But the site demonstrates that the BN-RRM framework can correctly learn from site-specific metal-pathway data when the fitting policy is not artificially suppressing it.
